signature_questions.what_they_press_on: China reopening significance, outbound China mix vs 2019, and ship deployment choices (e.g., Ovation moves).

topics.what_they_press_on: China reopening significance, outbound China mix vs 2019, and ship deployment choices (e.g., Ovation moves).
Operational ramp, pricing and return expectations for private destinations (Hideaway, CocoCay, Royal Beach Club / Perfect Day Mexico).

distinctive_concerns.blind_spots: He asks very little about fuel‑hedging strategy or hedging policy (0 turns on explicit fuel hedging despite peers discussing fuel in other calls). He also rarely presses on operational safety or specific health protocols after early 2022, even though peers raised those topics earlier.
distinctive_concerns.stance_drift: Early (2022-Q2/2022-Q3) his focus was on COVID protocol effects and apparent discounting; by 2023-Q3/2024-Q1 the emphasis shifted to yields, new‑ship impact and load factors (e.g., 2022-Q2 -> 2024-Q1). From 2023-Q3 to 2025-Q2 his questions increasingly covered private‑destination economics and capital allocation (dry docks/Hideaway -> Perfect Day Mexico/Royal Beach Club).

coverage_profile.seniority_signal: Title shown as Director / Senior Equity Research Analyst across the history; management routinely treats him as a repeat, knowledgeable questioner (multiple follow-ups taken in the same calls). This indicates a mid‑to‑senior sell‑side role with sustained coverage since 2022.
coverage_profile.sector_lens: Focused on cruise/leisure/travel with a consumer and revenue-management lens: questions center on yields, bookings cadence, mix effects (China, new ships), costs, and capital allocation.
rhetorical_signature: opens with a concise greeting and quick 'thanks' or 'congrats' (e.g. 'Hey, good morning, everybody')
structures questions as two‑part or multi‑part (setup + a pointed quant request) and often says 'just to follow up' or 'just to clarify'
tends to close turns with a brief acknowledgement ('Thanks', 'Perfect') and will frequently take a second turn for a clarification

reasoning_style.justification: He combines quantitative requests (explicit asks for mid‑single digits, EPS handles, percent/like‑for‑like impacts) with strategic framing (value gap to land vacations, new‑ship impact, channel/distribution). Example: 2022-Q2 asked about 'mid-single-digit net revenue per PCD' and 2024-Q1 probed China deployment strategy.
reasoning_style.follow_up_pattern: Multi‑part and follow‑up heavy — many turns are two‑part questions or include a specific follow-up ('A follow-up on Naftali's comments'). Rough fraction: ≈ 0.40 of turns contain ≥2 question segments (multi‑part or explicit follow‑up).
reasoning_style.evidence_demanded: He rewards explicit quantification and guidance (rates, EPS, percent booked). n_turns demanding explicit numerical quantification ≈ 10 (examples include 2022-Q2, 2022-Q3, 2023-Q3, 2024-Q2, 2024-Q4). n_turns using the word 'bps' = 0; he asks for basis‑point style precision but typically in percent or dollar handles.
reasoning_style.anchoring_habits: He repeatedly anchors to pre‑COVID/2019 baselines (e.g., 2022-Q2 and 2022-Q3 reference 2019), to the management line just delivered (e.g., 'mid-single-digit net revenue per PCD' in 2022-Q2), and occasionally to peer signals (e.g., 2025-Q1 referenced 'one of your larger peers').

queue_behavior.typical_position: #1–#6 across calls; distribution: #5 on 13 of 47 calls, #3 on 8 of 47, #4 on 8 of 47, #6 on 10 of 47, #2 on 6 of 47, #1 on 2 of 47.
queue_behavior.default_first_pick: When he is first/early he opens on big‑picture pricing or like‑for‑like yield questions (examples: 2024-Q3 asked about cumulative pricing versus 2019; 2024-Q4 opened on bridging yield guidance).

reframe_deeper.evidence: "what the mix shift alone would do"
"how to think about the likes, the embedded like‑for‑like"

white_space.evidence: "what the board needs to see to reestablish capital returns"

stay_on_topic_anyway.evidence: multiple follow ups on WAVE Season / pricing

examples.phrase: "A follow‑up on Naftali's comments in his prepared remarks"

cross_analyst_reactivity.operator_or_followup_signal: Takes a second turn in many calls for clarification or a deeper drill — observed taking multiple turns in ≈ 9 of the 15 calls (examples: 2022-Q2 had sequential follow ups; 2022-Q4, 2023-Q1, 2024-Q1 also show followups). When he takes a second turn it is usually for clarification or to reframe to a more quantitative angle.

framing_overrides_by_theme.booking_dynamics: Measures wave-season pace by brand and tests booked-position run vs peers.
framing_overrides_by_theme.capital_allocation_pattern: Frames capital return as a board-trigger question: what credit metrics unlock dividend/buyback.
framing_overrides_by_theme.yield_decomposition: Bridges midpoint guide to per-diem run-rate; isolates like-for-like inside guide and seasonality of Caribbean-weighted quarters.

_built_from: v7: company-layer split + probe/topic structure + v6 prose
